technology_decisions.description: Database for storing and querying model performance metrics over time

options.name: TimescaleDB
options.description: PostgreSQL extension for time-series data with continuous aggregates
options.recommendation_reason: We already use PostgreSQL — TimescaleDB adds time-series capabilities with minimal overhead

options.name: InfluxDB
options.description: Purpose-built time-series database with Flux query language
trade_offs: Better for pure metrics but adds operational complexity

technology_decisions.user_reason: Minimal new infrastructure — extends our existing PostgreSQL

technology_decisions.description: Frontend library for rendering real-time metric charts

options.name: Recharts
options.description: React-native charting library built on D3
trade_offs: Easy to use but limited for advanced visualizations
options.recommendation_reason: Best balance of ease-of-use and capability for dashboard charts

options.name: Apache ECharts
options.description: Powerful visualization library with extensive chart types

technology_decisions.user_choice_description: React-native charting library built on D3
technology_decisions.user_reason: React-native, good defaults, our data points per view stay under 2K

technology_decisions.description: Service for sending alert notifications via Slack and email

options.name: Direct API Integration
options.description: Direct calls to Slack API and SendGrid for email delivery
cons: More code for each new channel
trade_offs: Simple now but harder to scale to many channels
options.recommendation_reason: We only need Slack and email — a full notification service is overkill

options.name: Novu
options.description: Open-source notification infrastructure with multi-channel support
trade_offs: More capable but unnecessary complexity for our needs

technology_decisions.user_choice_description: Direct calls to Slack API and SendGrid
technology_decisions.user_reason: Keep it simple — Slack webhook + SendGrid is all we need

summary.estimated_learning_curve: Low — all chosen technologies are familiar or well-documented
